 {"id":"bd-6qd.5","title":"Create end-to-end release canary workflow for CLI and WASM artifacts","description":"Add release-canary smoke validation spanning CLI, WASM package, and web demo artifacts before publish.

Scope:
1. Build pre-release canary workflow that installs release artifacts in clean environments.
2. Run smoke commands for fm-cli parse/detect/render with sample corpus.
3. Run WASM package import/render checks in a minimal web project.
4. Validate web demo build/start/render path and capture screenshots/logs.
5. Publish a signed canary report artifact and fail release on unmet checks.

Why this improves the system:
- Catches packaging/distribution regressions before public release.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo check --all-targets, and relevant cargo test scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:43:30.014108243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39.226788066Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","cli","e2e","release","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39.226751177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-6qd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-6qd.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6qd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-6qd.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qxv","title":"XyChart: Implement dedicated layout and multi-series rendering","description":"# XyChart Full Implementation

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
xychart-beta
  title "Sales Revenue"
  x-axis [jan, feb, mar, apr, may, jun]
  y-axis "Revenue (in $)" 4000 --> 11000
  bar [5000, 6000, 7500, 8200, 9500, 10500]
  line [5000, 6000, 7000, 8000, 9000, 10000]
\`\`\`
- X-axis with categorical labels or numeric ranges
- Y-axis with label and min→max range
- Multiple series types: bar, line (on same chart)
- Chart title
- Auto-scaling to data range
- Grid lines

## Current State
- XyChart is marked UNSUPPORTED in the support table
- Parser does parse x-axis labels and series values (line/bar/area)
- No dedicated layout algorithm (falls through to Sugiyama which is wrong)
- No dedicated SVG rendering (no axes, no bars, no lines)

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout**: Add LayoutAlgorithm::XyChart variant. Compute chart area, axis positions, bar widths, line point positions from data values and axis ranges.
2. **SVG Renderer**: Draw axes with labels and tick marks. Draw bars as filled rectangles. Draw lines as <polyline> or <path>. Draw grid lines. Add title. Apply series colors from theme.
3. **Parser**: Ensure y-axis range parsing works (min --> max syntax). Parse title.
4. **Tests**: Bar chart, line chart, mixed bar+line, custom y-axis range, multiple x-axis labels.

## This moves XyChart from UNSUPPORTED to IMPLEMENTED.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.165005953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:33:09.233665056Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:33:09.233155021Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","xychart"],"comments":[{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-6qxv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:43Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-6qxv","author":"ubuntu","text":"INFRA NOTE: Unlike sequence/gantt which have separate infra beads, XyChart infra (IrXyChartMeta) should be built WITHIN this task because:
1. XyChart is simpler than sequence/gantt (no fragments, no dates)
2. The metadata is straightforward: title, x_labels, y_range, series_data
3. Creating a separate infra bead would over-fragment the work

Types to add to fm-core:
- IrXyChartMeta { title: Option<String>, x_axis: XyAxis, y_axis: XyAxis, series: Vec<XySeries> }
- XyAxis { label: Option<String>, categories: Vec<String>, min: Option<f32>, max: Option<f32> }
- XySeries { kind: XySeriesKind, values: Vec<f32> }
- XySeriesKind enum: Bar, Line, Area
- Add xy_chart_meta: Option<IrXyChartMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Bar chart with 6 categories → verify 6 bars with correct heights
2. Line chart with 6 data points → verify polyline connecting all points
3. Mixed bar+line → verify bars AND line render on same axes
4. Custom y-axis range 4000→11000 → verify axis labels and data scaling
5. Title → verify centered text above chart
6. Single data point → verify single bar or dot renders
7. Empty series → verify no crash, empty chart area
8. 100 data points → verify performance and readability","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6zd9","title":"Implement IrSequenceMeta for sequence diagram advanced features","description":"# Sequence Diagram IR Metadata

## Purpose
All advanced sequence diagram features (activation, notes, fragments, autonumber, participant groups) need structured metadata that doesn't fit in the generic node/edge model. This task adds a sequence-specific metadata structure to the IR.

## Types to Add
1. IrSequenceMeta struct containing:
   - autonumber: bool
   - activations: Vec<IrActivation> { participant: IrNodeId, start_edge: usize, end_edge: usize, depth: usize }
   - notes: Vec<IrSequenceNote> { position: NotePosition, participants: Vec<IrNodeId>, text: String }
   - fragments: Vec<IrSequenceFragment> { kind: FragmentKind, label: String, start_edge: usize, end_edge: usize, alternatives: Vec<FragmentAlternative>, children: Vec<usize> }
   - participant_groups: Vec<IrParticipantGroup> { label: String, color: Option<String>, participants: Vec<IrNodeId> }
   - lifecycle_events: Vec<IrLifecycleEvent> { kind: Create|Destroy, participant: IrNodeId, at_edge: usize }
2. NotePosition enum: LeftOf, RightOf, Over
3. FragmentKind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect
4. Add sequence_meta: Option<IrSequenceMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

## This unblocks: ALL sequence diagram advanced feature tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:41.054187839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:57:08.885800854Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T01:57:08.885280168Z","close_reason":"Implemented IrSequenceMeta and all associated types (NotePosition, FragmentKind, LifecycleEventKind, IrActivation, IrSequenceNote, FragmentAlternative, IrSequenceFragment, IrParticipantGroup, IrLifecycleEvent). Added sequence_meta: Option<IrSequenceMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr. 8 unit tests added covering serde round-trip, default values, JSON skip behavior, and enum variants.  {"id":"bd-75ja","title":"Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection","description":"## Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection

Use quotient filters (a cache-friendly alternative to Bloom filters) to detect duplicate edges during graph construction, enabling O(1) amortized duplicate detection with minimal memory overhead.

## Mathematical Foundation
A quotient filter stores a set of fingerprints compactly using quotienting: for hash h(x), split into quotient q and remainder r where h(x) = q * 2^r_bits + r. Store remainder r in slot q of a compact array, using linear probing for collisions. Properties:
- Space: ~10-25% less than Bloom filters at the same false positive rate
- Cache behavior: elements stored in contiguous sorted runs → excellent locality
- False positive rate: 2^{-r_bits} (configurable via remainder size)
- No false negatives: if element was inserted, it will be found

**For duplicate edge detection:**
When parsing a Mermaid diagram, each edge (u, v) must be checked for duplicates. For n edges:
- Naive HashSet: O(n) space, O(1) per lookup, but HashSet overhead per entry
- Quotient filter: O(n * r_bits / 8) bytes, O(1) per lookup, much lower constant factor
- For r_bits = 16: false positive rate = 1/65536, space = 2 bytes per edge
- For 10000 edges: quotient filter = ~20KB vs HashSet = ~400KB (20x savings)

**Practical consideration:** The space savings are significant for WASM target where memory is constrained. For native target, the HashSet overhead is negligible. This is primarily a WASM optimization.

## Key Papers
- Bender et al., "Don't Thrash: How to Cache Your Hash on Flash" (VLDB 2012) — quotient filter
- Pandey et al., "A General-Purpose Counting Filter" (SIGMOD 2017) — counting quotient filter
- Fan et al., "Cuckoo Filter: Practically Better Than Bloom" (CoNEXT 2014) — alternative approach

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Implement `QuotientFilter<H>` generic over hash function.
2. Use FxHash for fingerprint computation (fast, non-cryptographic).
3. Implement `insert(edge_hash)` and `may_contain(edge_hash)` operations.
4. On `may_contain() == true`: verify with exact edge comparison (handle false positives).
5. Replace `HashSet<(NodeId, NodeId)>` in parser with `QuotientFilter` for WASM target.
6. Keep `HashSet` for native target (simpler, negligible space overhead).
7. Benchmark: memory usage and parse time for 1000, 10000, 100000 edge graphs on WASM.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] QuotientFilter implementation with configurable false positive rate
- [ ] Duplicate edge detection correct (no false negatives, false positives handled)
- [ ] Memory reduction >= 10x vs HashSet for 10000-edge graphs
- [ ] Parse time comparable to HashSet (within 10%)
- [ ] WASM-specific optimization (cfg-gated)
- [ ] Property test: no false negatives over 100000 random edge sets
- [ ] Decision contract: adopt for WASM if memory reduction > 5x","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.205093497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:06:01.445943021Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:06:01.445547402Z","close_reason":"Implemented QuotientFilter in fm-core/src/quotient_filter.rs with: configurable q/r bit widths, FxHash-based fingerprinting, linear probing with sorted runs, insert/may_contain O(1) amortized operations, with_capacity auto-sizing (75% load target), memory/FP rate introspection. 14 unit tests all passing: basic operations, duplicates, edge tuples, stress testing (1000 elements, zero false negatives), empirical FP rate verification, memory comparison vs HashSet. All quality gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","parser","probabilistic","quotient-filter"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655486068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.362224276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-77gh","title":"Implement IrStyleDef and IrInlineStyle types in fm-core for styling support","description":"# Styling Infrastructure in fm-core

## Purpose
Multiple diagram types need styling support (classDef, style, linkStyle directives). This task creates the shared IR types that all styling features depend on.

## Types to Add
1. IrStyleDef { name: String, properties: BTreeMap<String, String> } — for classDef definitions
2. IrInlineStyle(BTreeMap<String, String>) — for style/linkStyle directives
3. Add style_defs: Vec<IrStyleDef> to MermaidDiagramIr
4. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrNode
5. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrEdge

## SVG Property Mapping
Document the mapping from CSS-like property names to SVG attributes:
- fill → rect/path fill attribute
- stroke → rect/path stroke attribute
- stroke-width → stroke-width attribute
- stroke-dasharray → stroke-dasharray
- color → text fill attribute
- font-size → font-size
- font-weight → font-weight

## Security
- Sanitize all property values to prevent SVG injection
- Reject url() values, javascript:, and event handlers

## This unblocks: classDef task, style/linkStyle task, and any diagram-specific styling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:40.857345936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:12:33.964746710Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:12:33.964452402Z","close_reason":"Implemented IrStyleDef, IrInlineStyle types in fm-core with populate_structured_styles(), sanitize_style_value(), parse_style_string() helpers, and 20+ unit tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-77gh","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Integration Tests

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

 {"id":"bd-79sj","title":"Implement class relationship cardinality and namespace grouping","description":"# Class Cardinality and Namespace Grouping

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  namespace Animals {
    class Dog
    class Cat
  }
  Dog "1" --> "*" Cat : chases
  Vehicle "1" *-- "0..*" Wheel
\`\`\`
- Cardinality labels: "1", "*", "0..1", "1..*", "0..*" on relationship ends
- Namespace blocks group classes visually (like packages in UML)
- Relationship types: inheritance (<|--), composition (*--), aggregation (o--), association (-->), dependency (..>), realization (..|>)

## Current State
- Cardinality labels on relationships: NOT parsed
- Namespace blocks: NOT parsed
- All relationship arrow types ARE parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse quoted cardinality labels before/after relationship arrows. Parse \`namespace Name { ... }\` blocks as clusters.
2. **IR**: Add source_cardinality/target_cardinality: Option<String> to IrEdge. Namespaces map to IrCluster.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Render cardinality labels near edge endpoints. Render namespaces as labeled boundary boxes.
4. **Tests**: Various cardinality labels, namespace grouping, nested namespaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.983780008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:01.062205308Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:01.061927472Z","close_reason":"Added source_cardinality/target_cardinality fields to IrEdge, parser strips quoted cardinality labels from class diagram edges, namespace blocks create subgraphs+clusters, SVG renderer draws cardinality text near edge endpoints. 6 new tests, all quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Class diagrams support a 'direction' keyword just like flowcharts:
```
classDiagram

```
The parser should recognize 'direction LR/RL/TB/BT' within classDiagram and set ir.direction accordingly. This is likely already handled by the generic direction parsing, but verify and add a test.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:51Z"}]}
